steps.title: Find the shared topic
steps.content: Text 1 discusses the word curry as a colonial term that some writers reject. Text 2 discusses a chef who recognizes that history but still calls some of his dishes curries. Both texts focus on whether and how the term is used.

steps.title: Separate topic from examples
steps.content: Choice A is too broad; the texts are not about the history of peoples on the subcontinent. Choice C is only an example in Text 1 and is absent from Text 2. Choice D invents a topic about research methods. The shared topic is the term curry. The answer is B.
